explanation.summary: The variable 'raw_value' is used before it is initialized.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided shows that the variable 'raw_value' is read before it is assigned a value.

fix_suggestions.title: Initialize the variable before using it
fix_suggestions.why: To prevent undefined behavior and unpredictable results.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Initializing the variable to a known value will ensure that it is not used before it is set.

fix_suggestions.title: Move the usage of 'raw_value' after its initialization
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure that the variable is always initialized before it is used.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Moving the usage of 'raw_value' after its initialization will prevent it from being used before it is set.

fix_suggestions.title: Add an initializer for 'raw_value'
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure that 'raw_value' is always initialized.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Initializing 'raw_value' to a known value will ensure that it is not used before it is set.

risk_analysis.why: The use of 'raw_value' before it is initialized can lead to undefined behavior and unpredictable results.
potential_failures: Unpredictable behavior in the program due to the use of an uninitialized variable.

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fix suggestions.
deviation_advice.required_justification: The use of an uninitialized variable can lead to unpredictable behavior and should be avoided.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all variables are initialized before they are used.

explanation.summary: The value of 'output' (uint16_t) is assigned to 'scaled' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value, causing data loss and incorrect program behavior.
explanation.rule_basis: The rationale is described in the introduction on composite operators and expressions (see Section 8.1 0.3). Casting to a wider type is not permitted as the result may vary between implementations. Consider the following: ( uint32_t ) ( u16a + u16b ); On a 16-bit machine the addition will be performed in 16 bits with the result wrapping modulo- 2 before it is cast to 32 bits. However, on a 32-bit machine the addition will take place in 32 bits and would preserve high-order bits that would have been lost on a 16-bit machine. Casting to a narrower type with the same essential type of the result always leads to the same loss of information.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided shows an implicit narrowing conversion from uint16_t to uint8_t.

fix_suggestions.title: Explicit cast
fix_suggestions.why: To avoid data loss and incorrect program behavior due to implicit narrowing conversion.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the value is correctly cast to the appropriate type.

fix_suggestions.title: Use a wider data type
fix_suggestions.why: To prevent data loss and incorrect program behavior due to implicit narrowing conversion.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the value is temporarily stored in a wider data type before being cast to the appropriate type.

fix_suggestions.title: Use a function to handle the conversion
fix_suggestions.why: To prevent data loss and incorrect program behavior due to implicit narrowing conversion.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the conversion is handled in a separate function, making the code easier to maintain and understand.

risk_analysis.why: The implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value, causing data loss and incorrect program behavior.

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fix suggestions.
deviation_advice.required_justification: Implicit narrowing conversion is not allowed according to MISRA2012-RULE-10_3.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that the fix does not introduce any new issues or unexpected behavior.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear inconsistent. The warning text mentions 'Narrowing conversion', while the retrieved context refers to 'Implicit narrowing conversion'.
